summary: Small matching/decomp pass over the Mr. Game & Watch Judge item file. The PR added several previously undecompiled or unexposed functions in itgamewatchjudge.c/h, including the Judge item spawn helper at 802C7774, orientation update helper at 802C78B8, animation/remove logic at 802C7B54, and moved existing functions to better match binary layout. The body explicitly notes that functions were reordered to match binary layout.
what_changed: Added includes needed for the new implementation: math.h, db/db.h, ft/ftlib.h, it/forward.h, and it/it_2725.h.
Implemented it_802C7774, a spawn helper for It_Kind_GameWatch_Judge using SpawnItem setup, Item_80268B18, Item_8026AB54, it_80273670, it_802C78B8, db_80225DD8, and it_8027CE64.
Implemented it_802C78B8 to orient a child JObj based on the owner fighter facing direction from ftLib_800865C0, setting rotation Y to M_PI when facing is 1.0f and 0.0f otherwise.
Moved itGameWatchJudge_Logic77_Destroyed earlier in the file to match the binary address/order and preserved its behavior of setting the Game & Watch judgment flag when the item has an owner.

Added itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim, which updates item orientation, checks ftGw_SpecialS_ItemCheckJudgementRemove, sets the judgment flag before removal when appropriate, and returns whether the item should be removed.
Moved itGameWatchJudge_Logic77_EvtUnk later in the file after the newly added animation function, consistent with the PR note about matching binary layout.
Updated itgamewatchjudge.h with prototypes and address comments for it_802C7774, it_802C78B8, and itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim.
smart_moves: Reordered functions to align source order with binary layout, making address-commented headers and future matching work easier to reason about.
Factored facing-dependent child JObj rotation into it_802C78B8 and reused the same orientation logic in the animation function, preserving observed behavior around owner facing.
Used explicit SpawnItem field initialization for the Judge item, including prev_pos/pos mirroring, kind, facing_dir, zero velocity, parent pointers, damage, and spawn flags.
Kept owner-null checks around Game & Watch special-state callbacks, avoiding unconditional calls into fighter-specific logic when the item has no owner.
Exposed new functions in the header with address comments matching the project's convention for item files.
decomp_lessons: For item source files, matching can require not only implementing missing functions but also reordering existing functions to follow the binary layout.
When decompiling spawn helpers, initialize SpawnItem fields in the order and style that matches the target, even for zeroed velocity and duplicated parent fields.
Game & Watch Judge item logic couples item lifecycle to fighter special-state flags; removal paths should preserve calls to ftGw_SpecialS_ItemJudgementSetFlag when an owner exists.
Facing-based model fixes may depend on ftLib_800865C0 and HSD_JObjSetRotationY with M_PI/0.0f rather than changing item facing directly.
Address comments in headers are useful anchors when adding prototypes for newly matched functions.
naming_conventions: Unidentified item functions retain address-style names such as it_802C7774, it_802C78B8, it_802C7A84, it_802C7AD0, and it_802C7AF0.
Logic callbacks use descriptive item-prefixed names such as itGameWatchJudge_Logic77_Destroyed and itGameWatchJudge_Logic77_EvtUnk.
The newly added animation callback is named itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim, preserving an uncertain/placeholder 'UnkMotion0' label.
Header prototypes include address comments such as /* 2C7774 */, /* 2C78B8 */, and /* 2C7B54 */.
Parameter names in the header use semantic labels where known, such as owner_gobj, bone_index, and judge_num, even when the C implementation uses more generic names like parent_gobj, part, and arg4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK(4) in it_802C7774 and PAD_STACK(8) in itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim, indicating stack-shape adjustments for matching.
Used a temporary Item* variable named new_var in itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim, likely to preserve compiler output or register/stack behavior.
Kept casts such as (float) (arg4 + 1), (Fighter_GObj*) parent_gobj, and (Item_GObj*) jobj as written in the matched code path.
Preserved exact float comparisons against 1.0f for facing direction and exact rotation constants M_PI and 0.0f.
Function ordering was deliberately adjusted to match binary layout, according to the PR body.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the supplied PR context.
The only human-text rationale captured was: 'reordered some funcs to match binary layout too'.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename it_802C7774, it_802C78B8, or itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim.
Check whether the duplicated facing-orientation logic between it_802C78B8 and itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim was later factored or renamed.
Search for other item PRs where functions were reordered to match binary layout.
Look for later review or cleanup around the placeholder variable name new_var in itGamewatchjudge_UnkMotion0_Anim.
Compare this Judge item spawn helper pattern against other Game & Watch item spawn helpers.
